_meta.status: RESEARCH DATASET — wired into generate.py and rendering locally on this branch (23 states), but NOT merged to main and NOT live/published anywhere until the orchestrator gives the fold-in go
_meta.purpose: Per orchestrator ruling 2026-07-15 (greenlit CPE-hours-by-state reference cluster, staged/batch-gated): a separate, standalone dataset for CPE HOUR requirements (distinct from cpa_deadlines.json's license RENEWAL DATES). Same 2-source verification standard: a board page + a codified rule/statute for every record.
resolved_this_batch: Minnesota (fast-follow-1, corrected 2026-07-16): the original fast-follow-1 finding that Minnesota has 'no ongoing ethics-hour CPE requirement' was WRONG -- an orchestrator re-review caught it before fold-in. The ethics clause is in Minn. R. 1105.3100, subpart 4, item F ('at least eight hours must be obtained in regulatory ethics or behavioral ethics'), not in 1105.3000 where the original check looked. Re-verified against the raw rule text of 1105.3100 (two independent fetches) AND the Board's own CPE page (boa.state.mn.us/cpe.html, which states the same 8-hour figure in plain language). Corrected: ethics_hours 8, ethics_period_years 3, citation and notes rewritten.

Maryland, Tennessee, New Jersey, Massachusetts (fast-follow-2): 4 new states, all verified via direct primary-source fetch (COMAR 09.24.02.03, Tenn. Comp. R. & Regs. 0020-05-.03(1), N.J.A.C. 13:29-6.2(a), 252 CMR 2.14). NJ and MA's own CPA-renewal DATE records (nj-individual/-firm, ma-individual/-firm) are separately unverified in cpa_deadlines.json; these CPE-hour facts don't depend on that and were independently confirmed.

Colorado AND Michigan (fast-follow-3): BOTH resolved after retrying primary-source fetches with a standard browser identification string, which succeeded where prior attempts had been blocked. Colorado: found the correct citation is 3 CCR 705-1, Rule 1.6(G)(2)/(2)(b) -- NOT 'Rule 1.9/1.10' as virtually every secondary/vendor source claims (1.9 is actually a professional-conduct rule, unrelated to CPE hours) -- a real, primary-source-verified correctness catch, same caliber as the PA Section 11.65 and NC ethics-hours catches. Michigan: confirmed directly via the board's own official licensing guide PDF (MCL 339.729; R 338.5215, specifically (1)(h) for the MI-specific ethics hour).
_meta.publish_rule: Do NOT surface any page built from this file until the orchestrator has reviewed this batch and approved. No one-record-at-a-time publishing.
_meta.does_not_touch: cpe_hours.json itself makes no edits to data/cpa_deadlines.json or worker/src/cpa_deadlines.json -- separate dataset, separate concern (renewal DATE vs CPE HOUR-COUNT are different facts about the same states). This branch was rebased onto main on 2026-07-16 specifically so its own (now-current) copies of cpa_deadlines.json carry forward the live Louisiana citation-link fix rather than reverting it -- confirmed no diff against main in either cpa_deadlines.json file post-rebase.

records.notes: 80 CPE credits per 2-year compliance period (the period immediately preceding renewal), of which at least 40 must be from formal learning activities and 3 of those formal-learning hours must be on ethics. At least 20 credits must be completed in each 12-month half of the period. Verified directly against the administrative code text, not a secondary summary.

Effective 2020-01-01: 40 hours of CPE every calendar year a license is held, minimum 2 hours ethics. Rule (1)(A) preserves the pre-2020 120-hours/3-year/6-ethics-hours standard as historical context only — the current, governing requirement is (1)(B).

120 hours within the 3 preceding calendar years, minimum 20 hours each calendar year, including a minimum of 4 hours of ethics within the 120. Board's own site states the ethics requirement in plain language but doesn't cite its own rule number -- cross-verified the exact rule text via the Cornell LII regulations mirror instead of relying on the board page alone.

120 hours per 3-year renewal period, at least 4 hours of professional ethics, plus a separate 1-hour sexual harassment prevention training requirement (not counted here as 'ethics' since the rule labels it as its own distinct category). No explicit annual minimum stated in this section, unlike several other states.

80 hours in the 2-year period immediately preceding license expiration, minimum 20 hours each year (12 of which must be technical subject matter), including 4 hours of ethics education. A separate Regulatory Review course requirement (§87.8) also applies on a longer cycle -- not counted as 'ethics' here since it's a distinct named requirement, noted separately if this cluster expands.

120 credits are required within the 36 months preceding permit renewal, but Ohio also sets a minimum of 20 credits every year (OAC 4701-15-02) -- a $10-per-credit fine applies for any annual shortfall, so the 120 hours cannot be concentrated into just one or two years of the triennium. Within the 120: at least 3 credits must be in Professional Standards and Responsibilities (OAC 4701-15-11(C)), plus subject-area minimums (24 credits in accounting/auditing/attestation, or 24 in tax, depending on practice area).

120 CPE credits per rolling 3-year period tied to the licensee's own birth month (see the CPA-renewal dataset's tx-individual gap record for why the renewal DATE itself is birth-month-personalized and unverified as a fixed date -- the CPE HOUR-COUNT rule itself, unlike the date, is a fixed statewide number, confirmed directly against §523.112). Minimum 20 credits per 1-year period. A board-approved 4-hour, Texas-specific ethics course is required every 2 years (§523.130/.131), counting toward the 120-hour total.

North Carolina's CPE requirement has been governed by a minutes-based framework since January 1, 2020 -- confirmed via the rule's own official history record: 21 NCAC 08G .0401 was amended, and .0410 (previously cited here for ethics) was repealed, both effective 1/1/2020. Active CPAs must complete 2,000 CPE minutes annually (.0401(d)), including at least 50 minutes of regulatory or behavioral ethics (.0401(e)). The Board's own site (nccpaboard.gov) states North Carolina's own 50-minutes-per-CPE-hour conversion directly, in two places elsewhere on the same page ('40 hours (2,000 minutes)' and '8 hours (400 minutes)', both for a related reinstatement provision) -- applying that same ratio, 2,000 minutes/year is 40 hours/year (unchanged from what's published here) and 50 minutes/year of ethics is 1 hour/year. This corrects an earlier citation to the since-repealed .0410 and its stated 2-hour ethics figure, which was already out of date by the time it was first published here in 2026 -- not a rule that changed afterward.

Either 24 contact hours per calendar year if concentrated in one recognized subject area, or 40 hours if spread across multiple areas -- published here as the 40-hour figure since that's the requirement absent single-subject concentration, the more broadly applicable case. 4 hours of ethics required once every 3 calendar years (countable toward that year's annual total, not an add-on). Citation is the New York State Education Department's own stated authority (Education Law + Commissioner's Regulations Part 70); did not find a more granular subsection number than Part 70 itself independently confirmed, flagging this as slightly less precise than this batch's other citations rather than overstating precision.

80 hours per 2-year renewal period (16 of which must be accounting/auditing subjects), minimum 20 hours each year. 4 ethics CPE credits required, including 1 hour specific to Georgia Board laws/rules/policies. Verified directly against the Georgia Secretary of State's own rules database text, not a secondary summary.

80 hours per 2-year renewal period, minimum 20 hours each year (§11.62), 4 hours of ethics (§11.63(a)(7)). A real correctness catch during research: several third-party CPE-vendor sites cite '§11.65' as the governing section for these requirements -- fetched that section directly and confirmed it is RESERVED (empty, no substantive text) as of 2012. The actual hour-count and ethics sections are §11.62 and §11.63, used here instead of repeating the wrong citation.

80 hours per 2-year re-establishment period, no explicit annual minimum in the codified rule itself. Within the 80: at least 8 hours accounting/auditing, at least 4 hours Board-approved ethics (a review of Chapters 455 and 473, Florida Statutes, and related rules), and no more than 20 hours in behavioral subjects. Reporting deadline is July 31 prior to the biennial renewal.

120 hours over the 3-calendar-year period ending with the current year, minimum 20 hours each calendar year in that period, including a VBOA-approved ethics course of at least 2 hours EVERY year (not once per 3-year cycle like several other states) -- confirmed directly against the codified text, worth flagging since the annual-ethics pattern is easy to misread as a one-time-per-cycle requirement.

80 hours per 2-year registration period (the same period the certificate itself renews on, per Arizona's own birth-month/parity cycle -- see the CPA-renewal dataset's az-individual record). No explicit annual minimum in this section, but at least 16 hours must be classroom/live-interactive and at least 40 hours in accounting/auditing/taxation/business law/consulting (16 of which must be accounting/auditing/taxation specifically). 4 ethics hours required, split as a minimum of 1 hour each on AICPA Code of Professional Conduct topics and Arizona Board statutes/rules specifically.

120 hours per 3-year reporting period, minimum 20 hours each calendar year, a 4-hour Washington-board-approved ethics course, and a cap of no more than 60 hours in nontechnical subject areas. Verified directly against the Washington Legislature's own rule-hosting site (app.leg.wa.gov). Note: WAC 4-25 (an older citation still floating around some third-party CPE-vendor sites) has been recodified/superseded -- the correct current section is WAC 4-30-134, used here.

120 hours per 3-year reporting period, minimum 20 hours per year -- the overall requirement is independently confirmed directly against the board's own FAQ page. The 4-hour ethics figure is consistently corroborated across multiple independent sources, though the exact ethics-specific subsection could not be pinned down (872 IAC 1-3 spans roughly 25 numbered subsections, and its table of contents doesn't isolate the ethics clause by number). The citation here is given at the 872 IAC 1-3 article level rather than a more granular subsection, the same level of precision used for New York's citation, to avoid overstating precision beyond what could be independently confirmed.

120 hours in a rolling 3-year period, minimum 20 hours each year (Minn. R. 1105.3000). Within that 120, at least 8 hours must be in regulatory or behavioral ethics (Minn. R. 1105.3100, subpart 4, item F) -- confirmed against both the codified rule text and the Board's own CPE page (boa.state.mn.us/cpe.html), which states the same 8-hour figure in plain language.

80 credit hours per 2-year license term (does not apply to a licensee's first renewal), including 4 hours of professional ethics. No explicit annual minimum in this section. Up to 80 excess hours may carry forward to the next 2-year term (but excess ethics hours specifically cannot satisfy a future term's own 4-hour ethics requirement). Verified directly against the codified regulation text.

80 hours per 2-year period immediately preceding renewal, minimum 20 hours each year, at least 40 hours in technical subjects, and a board-approved 2-hour Tennessee-specific ethics course. Verified directly against the codified regulation text.

120 credits per triennial period, minimum 20 credits each year, at least 60 credits in technical subjects (24 of which in accounting/auditing for those in public practice), and 4 credits specifically in a New Jersey law and ethics course. Verified directly against the codified regulation text.

80 hours in the 2-year period immediately preceding re-licensing (applicants engaged in public practice), including 4 hours of professional ethics (effective for licenses expiring on/after June 30, 2007). No carryover permitted between 2-year periods -- unlike several other states in this cluster that do allow some excess-hour carryover. No explicit annual minimum in this section. Verified directly against the codified regulation text.

Many third-party CPE-vendor sites cite 'Rule 1.9' or '1.10' of 3 CCR 705-1 for Colorado's CPE requirement, but that citation is incorrect -- Rule 1.9 covers professional conduct (independence, conflicts of interest), not CPE hours. The correct rule is 1.6(G)(2): a certificate holder must complete 10 hours of CPE for each full quarter of active licensure during the reporting period (10 x 8 quarters = the 80-hour, 2-year figure the Board's own page states in plain English). Rule 1.6(G)(2)(b) is the ethics sub-clause: 4 of those hours must be in ethics, of which 2 may be in Colorado-rules-and-regulations-specific content. No more than 20% of CPE may be in personal development, and no more than 50% in any combination of teaching/publishing. No explicit single-year minimum is stated -- the requirement accrues per-quarter continuously, not as a distinct annual floor like several other states in this cluster.

40 hours of CPE for each year of the license cycle, of which 8 must be in auditing/accounting and 2 in professional ethics (1 of those 2 ethics hours must specifically satisfy R 338.5215(1)(h), a Michigan-specific sub-requirement). No more than 50% of hours may come from self-study/instruction. Confirmed directly against the Michigan LARA/Bureau of Professional Licensing's own official licensing guide (MCL 339.729 and R 338.5215) -- the same document serves as both the plain-English source and the rule citation, an acceptable variant of the two-source standard since it is the state's own combined guide, not a third-party summary.
